# DeepInit — measured cost & time model 

*Generated by `tools/build_cost_model.py`. Every `$` is an ESTIMATE at claude-opus-4-8 list price ($5.0/$25.0 per Mtok, as of 2026-06-04; no [1m] premium). `output_tokens` is measured (budget delta); `input`/`$` are estimated (kemal in/out ratio) → **INDICATIVE**. A published figure needs a clean `api_usage` S/M/L run (the standing gate).*

## Per-tier estimation table (measured single-engine passes)

| Tier | n | source LOC | output tok | est total tok | est $ |
|------|---|-----------|-----------|--------------|-------|
| S | 2 | 1,095–5,820 | 18,495–19,892 | 150,808–162,199 | $1.12–$1.21 |
| M | 6 | 15,882–57,900 | 10,010–28,630 | 81,622–233,449 | $0.61–$1.74 |
| L | 1 | 105,175–105,175 | 24,381–24,381 | 198,803–198,803 | $1.48–$1.48 |

## Calibration

- kemal single-engine in/out ratio: **7.154** (input ≈ output × this).
- kemal actual/base (graphify-off): **14.32×** the bare LOC×1.2×1.8 forecast.
- matrix measured actual/base (graphify-on, discount 0.6): median **5.1×**, range [1.46, 106.28].
- actual/base uses the detection.md forecast base (graphify_discount 0.6 for the parseable matrix repos). A high ratio means the bare LOC×1.2×effort×0.6 formula UNDER-forecasts a real single-engine pass (it reads refs + reasons + re-reads). The forecast stays the source of truth, CALIBRATED by this factor.

## Method + caveats

- deterministic detection.md forecast across the full matrix + measured single-engine output-token cost on the run subset, input estimated via the kemal in/out ratio. token_source=count_tokens_estimate; publishable=INDICATIVE. A published $ still needs a clean api_usage S/M/L run.
- The single-engine pass is the cache-realistic LOWER bound; a thorough multi-component run on an L repo scales with component-count (the issue pass), not a single context — so L-tier here is a FLOOR.
- Lead with tokens; wall-time is environment-dependent and reported separately where measured.
